title: Deliciously Filipino Chicken Braised
description: A silky, post-workout Filipino dish featuring peppercorns and a lacquer-glaze, high in protein. Perfect for a busy day.

ingredients.name: chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: bone-in and skin-on for extra flavor

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the pot and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the peppercorns to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the chicken to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and brush the lacquer glaze all over it.
Return the chicken to the pot and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk and cook for 2 minutes.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with fresh herbs.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly and serving.
Serve with steamed rice or noodles, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
